label: ECDC — Measles overview
paragraphs: Measles is a serious and highly contagious viral disease. Symptoms of measles usually appear within 10-12 days of infection and can lead to complications and death.
The initial symptoms are:
runny nose
cough
mild fever
reddening of the eyes and sensitivity to light
In addition, between the third and the seventh day, the following symptoms may appear:
fever of up to 41 ⁰C
a red rash appears on the face, which lasts 4-7 days, and then spreads over the whole body
small white spots on the gums and inside the cheeks.
Read morefacts about measles

full_text:
Measles is a serious and highly contagious viral disease. Symptoms of measles usually appear within 10-12 days of infection and can lead to complications and death.

The initial symptoms are:

runny nose

cough

mild fever

reddening of the eyes and sensitivity to light

In addition, between the third and the seventh day, the following symptoms may appear:

fever of up to 41 ⁰C

a red rash appears on the face, which lasts 4-7 days, and then spreads over the whole body

small white spots on the gums and inside the cheeks.

Read morefacts about measles
